reason: Two findings (s4 and s5) have medium impact because the missing hints are canonical ref-label names that are the primary identifiers for the tags documented in those sections. Users searching by WebView_PrettyPrintTag / WebView_RawWriteTag (the exact form used in cross-references throughout the source) will not be routed to s4, and users asking about WebView_IncludeTag / WebView_IncludeParamTag (the primary subjects of s5) may miss that section. The Phase E fix agent did not resolve any of the four findings — the index/hints arrays in the Phase E output are byte-for-byte identical to the Phase D input, indicating the fix agent regenerated section content but left the index untouched.

findings_assessment.justification: WebView_ATag, WebView_ImgTag, WebView_ScriptTag, WebView_LinkTag are mentioned at the end of s3 as supplementary cross-references ('対応するカスタムタグ'), not as the primary subject. Their canonical documentation resides in dedicated sections. A user asking about these tags will still reach the authoritative content; they would only miss s3 if their query is specifically about URI specification combined with these tags. Section content itself contains the tag names, so semantic retrieval partially compensates.

The canonical ref-label forms WebView_PrettyPrintTag and WebView_RawWriteTag are the exact identifiers used in cross-references throughout the RST source and other documents. The existing hints use only the shortened forms PrettyPrintTag and RawWriteTag. An agent resolving a cross-reference ':ref:`WebView_PrettyPrintTag`' will search by the full canonical name and fail to match s4 hints, potentially missing the XSS-safety warnings that are critical for correct implementation. The inconsistency with Webview_WriteTag (which does use the full form) makes this a systematic gap.

WebView_IncludeTag, WebView_IncludeParamTag, and WebView_ConfirmationPageTag are the primary tags documented in s5 — the section exists specifically to explain how these tags support i18n resource path switching. Without these hints, a user asking 'how to use WebView_IncludeTag for multilingual support' will not be routed to s5. Unlike s3/s6 where tags appear in a supplementary list, these are the core subjects of the section.

WebView_SubmitTag, WebView_PopupSubmitTag, and WebView_DownloadSubmitTag appear in s6 only as items in a list of 'affected src attributes' for the cache-versioning feature. Their primary documentation is in dedicated sections. A user asking about cache control for static content will find s6 via existing hints (static_content_version, StaticResourceReload, キャッシュ). A user asking about WebView_SubmitTag specifically is unlikely to need s6 unless they already know about the src/cache relationship, in which case the section content itself provides the answer.
